rationale: The citing paper uses De Angelis et al. (2012) multiple times in its core theory section to justify that word of mouth can serve self-enhancement goals. It specifically relies on that paper for the claim that people may share both positive personal experiences and negative stories about others when self-enhancement is salient, and then uses this logic to motivate its cringe-sharing hypotheses. Although it does not reuse De Angelis et al.'s experimental design, the cited work plays a meaningful conceptual and empirical grounding role for the proposed mechanism.
relation_description: The citing paper treats De Angelis et al. (2012) as a conceptual foundation for its self-enhancement account of cringe sharing. It builds on that prior WOM logic to argue that sharing cringeworthy episodes enables favorable downward social comparison and thus self-enhancement.
